channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Wisconsin governor can lock in 400-year school funding increase using a veto, court says
item.description: The Wisconsin Supreme Court has ruled that the Democratic governor legally used the state’s uniquely powerful veto to lock in a school funding increase for 400 years.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Karen Read prosecutor wants to block defense from blaming after-party guests, pet dog in opening statement
item.description: Prosecution motion filed in Karen Read case to limit defense strategy to blame law enforcement or dog for Boston Police Officer John O'Keefe's death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma City to mark 30 years since the bombing that killed 168 people and shook America
item.description: A bomb with a force powerful enough to instantly destroy much of a nine-story building shattered a quiet Oklahoma City morning and sent a shock wave through America.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vertuno, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's claims about Abrego Garcia’s gang ties largely rely on 1 confidential tip
item.description: The Trump administration’s argument that the mistakenly deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia is a member of MS-13 is based largely on a confidential tip he and his family have long disputed.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Fyre Festival 2 'postponed' after Mexico plans derailed, ticket vendor issues refunds
item.description: Tickets are being refunded for Fyre Festival 2, which has been postponed just over a month before the event was scheduled to take place and weeks after organizers promised success.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Karen Read judge grants defense video access, allows dog bite experts – with limits
item.description: Judge issues key rulings in Karen Read murder retrial, granting defense access to surveillance video and allowing expert testimony on potential dog bites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, Fox 5

item.title: Officials demand answers as crews work to restore power after another Puerto Rico blackout
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Crews scrambled to restore power to Puerto Rico on Thursday after a blackout hit the entire island the previous day, affecting the main international airport, hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: Check your Venmo, email and mailbox: A payment from a years-old class action lawsuit against Facebook may be on its way this week.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meghan Markle has no staying power as podcast falls off top 10: Source
item.description: Meghan Markle's podcast, "Confessions of a Female Founder," made it to number 2 on Apple, but fell after one week.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Too cute! Surprise piglet born at Nashville Zoo
item.description: “He responded well to treatment and is now healthy and being hand-raised by our animal care team,” the zoo said.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 12-year-old Texas cancer patient using socks to help others
item.description: "You can wear it over the PICC lines and then it doesn't get caught as easily or and you can play more and stuff,” Abby said.
item.dc:creator: Caleigh Jo Hommel, NewsNation

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Donald Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Handyman named person of interest after missing woman's body found buried in concrete
item.description: A missing woman was found buried and partially encased in concrete more than a week after she went missing. Now, deputies are questioning her handyman for his possible role in her disappearance and death.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Outraged MrBeast fans seek refunds after Las Vegas event
item.description: A crowd of frustrated "MrBeast" fans gathered Tuesday in Las Vegas to demand refunds for a three-day experience. The Resorts World Las Vegas experience, which cost approximately $1,000, was held April 13-15 in partnership with the popular YouTuber and entrepreneur, also known as Jimmy Donaldson.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, KTLA 5

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police in a Boston suburb have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wink Martindale, game show host and Elvis Presley interviewer, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the host of game shows who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died at 91.
item.dc:creator: By BETH HARRIS Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why Utah’s wetlands around the Great Salt Lake may matter more than you think
item.description: Wetlands around the Great Salt Lake are vital to the health of Utah's ecosystem — but did you know that wetlands can also filter out pollutants in the water?
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Video shows ICE agents shatter car window during Massachusetts arrest: attorney
item.description: Federal immigration agents are accused of shattering a car window in Massachusetts while detaining a Guatemalan man on Monday in a caught-on-camera incident – despite his attorney saying they had the wrong person.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, WGN 9

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, Fox 5

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawyer for detained Palestinian activist says client is relieved he hasn't been moved from Vermont
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A Palestinian man arrested at a Vermont immigration office during an interview about finalizing his U.S. citizenship is relieved that he hasn't been moved out of state and thankful for those supporting him, his attorney said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shapiro arson attack underscores threats to politicians
item.description: The arson attack at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s (D) residence is the latest incident to underscore the threats faced by high-profile politicians amid the country’s political polarization.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, ABC 4

item.title: Seven US service members had ‘COVID-19-like symptoms’ after 2019 Wuhan games: Pentagon report
item.description: Seven U.S. service members exhibited “COVID-19-like symptoms” during or after their return from the 2019 World Military Games in Wuhan, China, according to a Pentagon report recently made public.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
